Title: PII interactions with the acetyl-CoA carboxylase subunits BADC and BCCP co-regulate lipid and nitrogen metabolism in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-regulates seed oil, seed protein, leaf lipid/protein balance, seedling htACCase activity, and nitrite utilization through interactions with ACCase regulatory/catalytic subunits
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of PII in badc1badc2 or badc1badc3 backgrounds reverted the elevated seed oil phenotype of badc double mutants back toward WT. 2. Loss of PII in either badc background reduced seedling htACCase activity by 46% or 38%, respectively. 3. PII loss partially reversed badc-dependent changes in seed protein, leaf lipid/protein content, nitrite sensitivity, and ACCase subunit stoichiometry.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and nitrogen storage reserves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Quantified in developing seeds; function assessed in seedlings, leaves, and seeds
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NAGK; BADC1; BADC3; BCCP2; BC; weak/minimal with BADC2; weak/minimal with BCCP1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants and stacked triple mutants; split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid; seed/leaf metabolite profiling; nitrite survival assay; htACCase activity assay; AQUA-MRM absolute proteomics
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High nitrogen fertilization; low nitrogen fertilization; nitrite as sole nitrogen source; growth light regime discussed
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of PII in badc backgrounds reduced rosette/root biomass and reduced seedling htACCase activity; altered nitrogen metabolism
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PII is more abundant than all BADC and BCCP isoforms combined in developing seeds. The paper concludes PII is not a simple negative regulator; instead it selectively binds BADC/BCCP isoforms and modulates ACCase stoichiometry and nitrogen metabolism in an N-dependent manner.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for balancing seed oil and protein under defined nitrogen regimes, but manipulation may cause growth and nitrogen-metabolism tradeoffs; should be combined cautiously with ACCase subunit engineering

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Biotin/lipoyl attachment domain-containing non-biotinylated ACCase regulatory subunit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with other BADC isoforms, modulates seed oil, seed protein, leaf lipid composition, and nitrogen-related phenotypes via htACCase regulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BADC1 is a shared mutated component in both badc1badc2 and badc1badc3 lines that show elevated seed oil and seed protein under high N. 2. BADC1 shows the strongest interaction with PII among BADCs, supporting an important regulatory role. 3. Loss of BADC1-containing combinations alters ACCase subunit stoichiometry and PII-dependent metabolic outputs.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: One of the most abundant BADC isoforms in seeds according to study discussion/proteomics context
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed in developing seeds via absolute proteomics context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant and triple-mutant genetic analysis; split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid; proteomics; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss in double-mutant backgrounds associates with altered vegetative growth, altered leaf lipid composition, and nitrite sensitivity depending on remaining isoform
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although BADCs were previously described as negative regulators, this paper supports a more nuanced role in htACCase assembly/rheostat-like regulation and crosstalk with nitrogen metabolism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation of BADC composition may increase seed oil and protein, but effects are isoform-specific and PII- and nitrogen-dependent

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Biotin/lipoyl attachment domain-containing non-biotinylated ACCase regulatory subunit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence as the remaining BADC isoform in badc1badc3 likely supports relatively active htACCase regulation with modest vegetative phenotypes and elevated seed oil/protein under high N
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In badc1badc3, BADC2 is the only remaining BADC isoform and is associated with increased seed oil and protein under high N. 2. PII showed minimal to no direct interaction with BADC2 in yeast. 3. The badc1badc3 phenotypes and their reversion by pii indicate BADC2-dependent regulation is still modulated indirectly through PII interactions with other subunits such as BCCP2.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Discussed as potentially important in vegetative tissues; co-regulated with circadian genes per cited literature
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inferred from mutant phenotype analyses in leaves, seedlings, and seeds
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Minimal to no interaction with PII in yeast
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant/triple-mutant analysis; yeast two-hybrid; seed/leaf metabolite assays; proteomics
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Isoform-specific effects on ACCase activity and reserve accumulation may depend on environmental conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes BADC2-containing BC-BCCP-BADC complexes may be among the more active combinations in vitro, helping explain modest vegetative defects in badc1badc3.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Isoform engineering of BADC2-containing complexes may enhance seed oil while maintaining vegetative performance, but requires consideration of PII and N status

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: CS2103834-derived mutant background; exact locus NA
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Biotin/lipoyl attachment domain-containing non-biotinylated ACCase regulatory subunit
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence as the remaining BADC isoform in badc1badc2 is associated with stronger vegetative defects, altered leaf lipid/protein and nitrite phenotypes, and elevated seed oil/protein under high N
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In badc1badc2, BADC3 is the only remaining BADC isoform and the mutant shows reduced vegetative biomass, altered leaf lipid/protein, increased nitrite sensitivity, and increased seed oil/protein. 2. BADC3 interacts moderately with PII in yeast. 3. badc1badc2 seeds show increased BADC3, α-CT, and PII abundance, suggesting compensatory adjustment of htACCase stoichiometry.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in developing seeds by absolute proteomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function inferred from seedling, leaf, and seed phenotypes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant/triple-mutant analysis; split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid; htACCase activity assay; proteomics; seed/leaf metabolite assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High versus low nitrogen fertilization; nitrite treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Association with reduced rosette and root growth, altered nitrogen metabolism, and reduced leaf lipid content in badc1badc2 background
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study proposes BADC3-containing complexes may have intermediate activity and require compensatory increases in other ACCase subunits in seeds.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating BADC3-associated complexes may alter seed oil and protein, but stronger vegetative and nitrogen-related tradeoffs are likely

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Biotin carboxyl carrier protein subunit of heteromeric ACCase
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A non-catalytic carrier subunit whose abundance and selective interaction with PII are linked to altered htACCase stoichiometry and seed oil phenotypes in badc mutant backgrounds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PII interacts more strongly with BCCP2 than BCCP1 in yeast. 2. BCCP2 abundance decreased in badc1badc3 and was partially restored in piibadc1badc3. 3. Selective PII-BCCP2 interaction is proposed to contribute to differential regulation of htACCase activity and oil accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid; AQUA-MRM absolute proteomics; genetic interaction analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High versus low nitrogen context for phenotypic interpretation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes in abundance may reflect compensatory control limiting excess fatty acid synthesis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated here as a core common gene because its role is structural/carrier/regulatory within htACCase rather than direct catalysis of a distinct metabolic step.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for tuning ACCase complex composition and oil accumulation, likely requiring coordinated engineering with PII/BADCs
